raw_consumer_statement:
This letter is in response to your XX/XX/XXXX communication regarding my continued fraud dispute on XXXX unauthorized transactions posted on XX/XX/XXXX for {$320.00} and XX/XX/XXXX for {$2700.00}, from AP * XXXX XXXX STORE. These transactions were not authorized by me and were initially reported as fraud in XX/XX/XXXX. 

Despite my repeated reassertions of this fraud claimon XX/XX/XXXX ; XX/XX/XXXX ; XX/XX/XXXX ; XX/XX/XXXX ; XX/XX/XXXX ; and again through multiple formal dispute filingsBarclays has continuously failed to provide any legitimate proof of cardholder authorization. Simply citing deferred interest promotions and minimum payment activity does not establish that I approved or recognized these charges. 

These transactions were and are fraudulent : - I did not initiate or authorize the purchases from the XXXX XXXX XXXX. 
- I never received the merchandise, did not log in or check out from the XXXX platform, nor did I sign for or approve the transactions. 
- Your institution has not provided transaction authorization logs, shipping confirmation, device/IP verification, or any conclusive fraud investigation documentation. 

Failure to Comply with XXXX : Under the Fair Credit Billing Act ( 15 U.S. Code 1666 ) and Regulation Z ( 12 CFR 1026.13 ), Barclays was required to : 1. Investigate my dispute fairly and thoroughly, which must include a good faith effort to validate cardholder authorization.

2. Suspend collection efforts during an active dispute, including interest accrual.

3. Respond with supporting documentation when a claim is deniednot generalizations or procedural assertions.

Reasserting a dispute does not waive my FCBA rights. If anything, it proves I have continuously contested these charges and have not accepted your conclusions. You can not simply declare my claim final without a legally compliant investigation or providing me the results of such an inquiry. 

Action Demanded : 1. Immediately reverse the charges of {$320.00} and {$2700.00} and issue corresponding interest and fee credits. 
2. Provide all documentation Barclays used to validate these charges, including authorization records, shipping logs, billing and delivery address details, and any correspondence or evidence used to deny my claims. 
3. Cease collection activity related to these charges unless and until Barclays complies with its obligations under federal law. 

This matter remains open and active with the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau. I have also engaged legal counsel, who is currently evaluating Barclays ' repeated failure to respond in good faith and its potential violations of consumer credit laws. Please consider this your final notice before further legal escalation.
